breaking.title: ‘People can be killed but ideals can't’: Iran condemns Trump's 'one shot' threat
breaking.summary: Iran has fiercely condemned US President Donald Trump's remarks about potentially eliminating its leadership, accusing America of lacking "civilisation, history, or honour." Trump's comments, made in an interview, questioned the sincerity of mourners at the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and suggested a swift strike could eliminate Iran's top officials. The funeral itself has drawn massive crowds, projected by Iran as a display of national unity and defiance.

breaking.title: Shooting in US on fourth of July: 8, including 4 children, injured in Brooklyn's Coney Island
breaking.summary: Tragedy struck Coney Island on Friday night as a shooting left at least eight people injured, including four young children. The incident occurred around 10:37 pm, with victims ranging from six to 37 years old. While most are in stable condition, a 21-year-old woman is critically injured. Police have recovered a firearm but no arrests have been made as investigations continue into the disturbing event.

breaking.title: V. Sivankutty urges Kerala government to resolve administrative paralysis at Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
breaking.summary: Sivankutty alleges that attempts by NDA-led Corporation’s leadership to protect Vazhottukonam ward councillor R. Sugathan, who is under judicial remand after being arrested under provisions of Kerala Anti-Social Activities Prevention Act, has disrupted day-to-day functioning of the local body

breaking.title: Where is Mojtaba? Iran's new Supreme Leader skips father Ali Khamenei's funeral
breaking.summary: During the state funeral prayers for his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and four family members, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, chose to remain out of the public eye. Citing security threats following an attack, his absence has sparked considerable speculation. As his brothers and key officials attended, eyes are turned towards Mojtaba's anticipated return amidst reports of personal injuries and ongoing security concerns.

breaking.title: "Mentally Unstable": Government Sources Slam Pak's Khawaja Asif For Attack On PM Modi
breaking.summary: Government sources said Pakistan's criticism reflected "envy" rather than facts.

breaking.title: Cargo ship under attack 'by unknown armed assailants' in Red Sea, says British military
breaking.summary: A cargo ship faced an attack in the Red Sea near Yemen's coast, the UK military confirmed Sunday. The vessel reported being targeted by unknown armed individuals. Investigations are underway, with no immediate details on the ship or any damage. While Houthi rebels have previously threatened such actions, no group has claimed responsibility yet. The incident raises concerns amid recent pirate activity in the Gulf of Aden.

breaking.title: Evacuations in Guam as super typhoon Bavi approaches
breaking.summary: The storm is forecast to bring winds in excess of 160mph and waves nearly 11m high when it makes landfall on Monday.

breaking.title: Another Monsoon Death In Mumbai, Man Dies After Tree Falls On Him Amid Rain
breaking.summary: Yunus Hakimuddin Sheikh was killed when a tree fell on him
